problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Psychedelic-induced. Psychedelic-induced neuroplasticity via 5-HT2A receptor directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Longitudinal patient cohort with 6 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The important thing is not to stop questioning. — Einstein
